Photorealistic UE5 RTX render of massive mechanical manufacturing empire showing FOUR DIFFERENT PRODUCTION TYPES simultaneously in one complex.

FOREGROUND (20% depth) - JOB SHOP CUSTOM PRODUCTION:
Three gnome craftsmen at individual wooden workbenches. GNOME #1: Hammering custom brass gear on anvil, sparks flying, focused expression, leather apron, safety goggles. GNOME #2: Measuring precision component with brass calipers, squinting, technical drawing on desk. GNOME #3: Assembling intricate clockwork mechanism with tiny screwdrivers, magnifying glass mounted on stand. Workbenches covered with hand tools (hammers, files, wrenches), blueprints rolled and pinned, prototype parts, warm forge lighting from left, intimate workshop atmosphere.

MID-GROUND LEFT (40% depth) - BATCH PRODUCTION:
Circular production cell with 6 workstations arranged in ring. Elf workers in matching uniforms rotating through stations in synchronized rhythm. STATION 1: Molding clay forms in wooden molds. STATION 2: Loading pieces into brick kiln, fire glowing inside. STATION 3: Glazing finished ceramics with brushes. STATION 4: Quality inspection with magnifying glass, checking for defects. STATION 5: Wrapping in paper, packing in wooden crates. STATION 6: Labeling with stamps, logging in inventory ledger. Wooden conveyor belt connecting stations in continuous loop, rhythmic cyclical movement, medium industrial lighting.

MID-GROUND RIGHT (40% depth) - MASS PRODUCTION LINE:
Long assembly line stretching into distance with 20+ dwarf workers at individual stations. Wide leather conveyor belt moving continuously left to right with metal products. Workers performing repetitive tasks: STATION A: Attaching component A. STATION B: Welding joints (sparks, protective masks). STATION C: Applying paint with spray apparatus. STATION D: Drying under heat lamps. STATION E: Final packaging. Steam-powered mechanical arms (brass and copper construction, Victorian aesthetic) assisting workers with heavy lifting. Hypnotic synchronized efficiency, cool industrial lighting from overhead, metallic surfaces reflecting light.

BACKGROUND (80% depth) - CONTINUOUS FLOW REFINERY:
Vast network of pipes, tanks, and valves. Transparent glass pipes showing liquid metal flowing (glowing orange-gold molten material). Large cylindrical tanks with pressure gauges. Steam venting from brass pressure relief valves. Goblin engineers in protective gear monitoring gauges, adjusting valve wheels, taking readings on clipboards. Pipes running vertically and horizontally creating complex 3D network. Never-stopping 24/7 operation. Dramatic backlighting from molten materials creating atmospheric glow, silhouettes of workers against bright furnaces.

INTERCONNECTIONS THROUGHOUT:
Overhead crane system with hooks and chains moving materials between zones. Physical wooden and metal conveyors crossing between production areas. Raw materials entering from left side (carts, barrels, crates). Finished goods exiting right side on loading dock. Central elevated control tower (wooden structure, 3 stories) with supervisor fox character overseeing all four processes, holding speaking tube, pointing at different areas.

ATMOSPHERE: Organized industrial symphony, each production type with distinct visual rhythm, clear workflow from raw materials to finished products, sense of scale and complexity, physically-based mechanical systems only (no digital displays), natural materials (wood, stone, metal, brick), steampunk-industrial aesthetic, warm and cool lighting zones creating depth, active workers performing real tasks, visible cause-and-effect in all processes.

CAMERA: Wide establishing shot showing all four production types simultaneously, slight elevated angle to see layout, deep depth of field showing foreground detail and background scale, cinematic composition with strong leading lines from conveyors and pipes.

Tools scattered: precision screwdrivers, small wrenches, soldering iron with smoke rising, calipers, magnifying glass on stand. MID-GROUND - CHEMICAL/MATERIAL EXPERIMENTS BENCH:
Long laboratory bench (12ft) with 10 different experiments running simultaneously:

- EXPERIMENT 8: Test tube rack with 20 samples in different colors
- EXPERIMENT 9: Petri dishes with cultures growing
- EXPERIMENT 10: Electrolysis apparatus with electrodes in solution

Five mouse chemist characters in white coats moving between experiments, monitoring progress, adjusting temperatures with valve controls, adding reagents with droppers, recording observations in notebooks. Periodic table chart on wall. Safety equipment (goggles, gloves) on hooks.

BACKGROUND LEFT - PATENT DOCUMENTATION OFFICE:
Three squirrel clerk characters at drafting tables drawing detailed technical diagrams with precision pens and rulers. Large sheets of parchment with mechanical drawings, measurements, annotations. Filing cabinet system with labeled drawers ("Approved Patents", "Pending Review", "In Development"). Rubber stamps for approval ("PATENT GRANTED"). Rolled blueprints in storage tubes on shelves. Legal documents being reviewed. Organized, bureaucratic atmosphere. Desk lamps providing focused lighting.

Enclosed testing area (8ft x 8ft) with reinforced glass walls for safety. Mechanical device being stress-tested inside - visible strain on components. Pressure test equipment applying force. Durability test with hammer mechanism striking repeatedly. Performance test with device operating at high speed. Two engineer characters (dwarf and elf) outside chamber with clipboards recording failure points, structural weaknesses, performance data. Crash test dummy-style setup for safety testing. CEILING - IDEA GENERATION MOBILE:

Occasionally new bulb lighting up (moment of discovery). Gentle rotation creating dynamic shadows. Whimsical yet purposeful design.

ATMOSPHERE: Bright, clean laboratory lighting (5500K color temperature), organized chaos of active research, sense of discovery and innovation, multiple breakthroughs happening simultaneously, scientific method in action, collaboration between different species, excitement of experimentation, professional yet creative environment, steampunk-Victorian scientific aesthetic, brass and glass equipment, wooden furniture, parchment documentation.

LIGHTING: Bright overhead skylights providing even illumination, task lamps at individual stations, warm glow from Bunsen burners, colored light from chemical reactions, dramatic lighting from testing chamber, soft glow from idea bulbs above.

CAMERA: Wide establishing shot from slightly elevated angle showing entire laboratory, deep depth of field capturing foreground detail and background context, balanced composition with multiple focal points of activity.

Photorealistic UE5 RTX render of elaborate hydraulic financial system visualizing money flow as physical liquid through transparent pipes.

CENTER - CAPITAL RESERVOIR:
Large cylindrical glass tank (8ft diameter, 10ft tall) filled with glowing golden liquid (representing money/capital). Liquid has magical luminescent quality, casting warm golden light. Tank has brass fittings, pressure gauges on sides, level indicators showing current capital amount. Transparent glass allows viewing liquid inside. Tank mounted on wooden platform with stairs for access.

LEFT SIDE - ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE (INFLOW SYSTEM):
Four input pipes (4-inch diameter, transparent glass with brass joints) bringing golden liquid into reservoir from left. Each pipe has different color-coded label band: BLUE pipe labeled "Product Sales" (largest flow), GREEN pipe labeled "Service Contracts", PURPLE pipe labeled "Subscriptions", ORANGE pipe labeled "Investments". At each pipe: Gnome accountant in green visor and vest with clipboard recording incoming amounts, checking flow meter (brass gauge showing volume). Flow meters with spinning dials. Check valves (brass, one-way) preventing backflow. Mesh filters catching debris (representing "bad debt") - visible particles caught in screens. Gnome workers cleaning filters. Liquid flowing with visible current and bubbles.

RIGHT SIDE - ACCOUNTS PAYABLE (OUTFLOW SYSTEM):
Five output pipes (varying diameters) distributing golden liquid from reservoir to right. Each pipe controlled by large brass valve wheel. Dwarf treasurers in formal vests at each valve, carefully turning wheels to regulate flow. Pipes labeled and color-coded: RED pipe "Payroll" (largest, 6-inch diameter), BROWN pipe "Operations" (4-inch), GRAY pipe "Rent/Facilities" (3-inch), YELLOW pipe "Marketing" (3-inch), SILVER pipe "R&D" (2-inch). Each valve has padlock requiring authorization. Dwarf checking authorization papers before opening valves. Liquid flowing through at controlled rates.

CENTER ELEVATED - FINANCIAL ANALYSIS STATION:
Owl analyst character (wearing round spectacles and green accountant's visor) standing on elevated wooden platform (8ft high) overlooking entire system. Platform has brass railing. Owl holding quill pen and ledger book. Multiple monitoring instruments on platform: Large brass pressure gauge labeled "Cash Flow Pressure" with red/yellow/green zones. Glass thermometer labeled "Market Temperature". Brass speedometer labeled "Burn Rate" with needle. Large balance scale labeled "Assets vs Liabilities" with weights on each side. Giant wooden abacus (3ft wide) with brass beads for calculations. Owl actively moving beads, taking notes, monitoring gauges with concerned expression. Stack of ledger books nearby. Oil lamp providing focused lighting on workspace.

BOTTOM - AUDITING & COMPLIANCE:
Three badger auditors in formal black suits with magnifying glasses inspecting pipes for leaks. BADGER #1: Examining pipe joint closely, looking for cracks. BADGER #2: Testing liquid sample in glass beaker, comparing color to reference chart. BADGER #3: Checking certification seals on valves, holding clipboard with compliance checklist. Small workbench with testing equipment (beakers, test tubes, reference manuals). Flashlight illuminating inspection areas.

TOP - BUDGETING & FORECASTING:
Elevated control room (wooden structure, glass windows) above main tank. Two strategic planner characters (fox in business attire) reviewing future plans. Large planning board with chalk drawings showing projected flows. Adjustable valve settings for future periods. Weather vane on roof showing "Market Conditions" (currently pointing to "Moderate Growth"). Telescope pointed at horizon for forecasting.

BACKGROUND:
Vault doors (heavy metal, circular locking mechanism) showing reserves storage. Safe deposit boxes visible through open vault. Gold bars stacked (representing physical assets). Stone walls suggesting secure facility.

INTERCONNECTIONS:
All pipes connected to central reservoir. Visible liquid flowing with realistic fluid dynamics. Pressure equalization between inflow and outflow. Overflow pipe for excess capital. Emergency drain valve. Maintenance ladders for accessing high pipes.

ATMOSPHERE: Sophisticated financial machine, money as tangible flowing resource, complete transparency of all transactions, careful monitoring and control, sense of balance and regulation, steampunk-Victorian aesthetic with brass and glass, warm golden lighting from liquid, cool shadows in background, professional banking environment, visible cause-and-effect of financial decisions.

LIGHTING: Primary light source from glowing golden liquid in pipes and tank, secondary warm lighting from oil lamps, dramatic shadows creating depth, brass surfaces reflecting golden light, glass transparency showing internal flow.

CAMERA: Straight-on view showing complete system from left (inflow) through center (reservoir and analysis) to right (outflow), slight elevation to see top control room, composition emphasizing symmetry and balance of financial system.

- DESK 5: Human agent sorting incoming tickets by colored priority flags attached (RED flag=Urgent, YELLOW=Normal, GREEN=Low priority)

Overhead pneumatic tube network (transparent glass tubes, brass fittings) with tickets traveling through tubes, arriving at appropriate desks. Tickets dropping into desk inboxes with soft thud. Wooden ticket bins on each desk labeled by category: "Technical Support", "Billing Questions", "Product Information", "General Inquiry". Conveyor belt system at back removing resolved tickets to archive room. Warm, welcoming lighting (3500K). Professional but friendly atmosphere.

MEZZANINE LEVEL (20-40%) - LIVE CHAT SUPPORT:

Status board with operator names and "Available/Busy" indicators (green/red flags). Focused, fast-paced energy.

SECOND FLOOR (40-60%) - KNOWLEDGE BASE LIBRARY:

Three owl librarian characters with spectacles FLYING between shelves, retrieving information scrolls and books, delivering to agents below via dumbwaiter system. Card catalog system (wooden drawers with brass handles) for quick reference lookup. Two squirrel scribes at desks continuously updating library, writing new articles, adding to collection. Scrolls, leather-bound manuals, organized filing. Warm library lighting, scholarly atmosphere.

THIRD FLOOR (60-80%) - CUSTOMER FEEDBACK PROCESSING:

ANALYSIS TEAM (3 characters) at separate table with hand-drawn charts tracking sentiment trends over time (line graphs on large parchment, colored ink). Compiling feedback into reports using typewriters. Feedback being bound into quarterly report books.

Physical connection visible: rope pulley system sending insight reports to R&D department (rope going through wall to adjacent building). Integration with product development. Organized, systematic processing.

FOURTH FLOOR (80-95%) - CUSTOMER SUCCESS & ONBOARDING:
VIP lounge atmosphere with comfortable seating, elegant decor. CONCIERGE TEAM (4 characters in formal attire) providing white-glove service to high-value customers (represented by well-dressed character guests).

ONBOARDING SPECIALISTS (3 characters) at welcome desks walking new customers through setup process, showing product samples, demonstrating features with physical models. Training materials spread on tables: welcome kits being prepared (boxes with ribbons), instruction manuals, product samples, welcome letters with wax seals.

Success metrics board (large wooden board) showing customer health scores with colored pins on chart (green=healthy, yellow=at-risk, red=needs attention). PROACTIVE OUTREACH TEAM (2 characters) writing check-in messages, preparing care packages. Warm, luxurious atmosphere with plants, artwork, premium feeling.

ROOFTOP (95-100%) - CENTRAL COORDINATION TOWER:
Glass-enclosed control room at top with OPERATIONS MANAGER (fox in executive attire) overlooking entire complex through windows. Elevated position providing overview of all five floors below. Manager holding speaking tube connected to all floors, pointing at different levels, directing operations.

Signal flag system (colored flags on poles) communicating between floors: Blue=Normal operations, Yellow=High volume, Red=Emergency escalation. Priority escalation rope-pull system (like old fire alarm) for urgent issues.

Large performance dashboard (wooden board with mechanical counters, brass dials):

CENTRAL COORDINATION SPINE (Vertical, through all floors):
Spiral wooden staircase connecting all five floors, agents moving up and down. Central pneumatic tube shaft running vertically with tubes branching to each floor. Rope pulley system (like dumbwaiter) transporting documents between levels. Emergency bell system for urgent communications. Architectural feature tying all floors together.

EXTERIOR CONNECTIONS (Visible at edges):

text: ¡Excelente! Las 5 ventanas están creadas. Ahora voy a generar el retrato profesional de Foxy para el feed de webcam:

artifact.prompt_text:
Photorealistic portrait of FOXY - stunning professional blonde woman with elegant fox ears, webcam-style framing for IDE interface.

FOXY'S APPEARANCE:
Beautiful blonde woman, mid-30s, photorealistic Unreal Engine 5 quality. Elegant fox ears (subtle, sophisticated, blonde fur matching hair, alert and forward-facing showing engagement). Face: Gorgeous, intelligent, confident expression with slight professional smile. Eyes: Bright, focused, looking slightly upward (as if viewing multiple windows above). Mouth: Slightly open as if speaking/teaching. Skin: Flawless, professional makeup, warm lighting. Hair: Perfectly styled blonde hair, shoulder-length, professional yet glamorous, slight wave. Body: Attractive, bustier figure, professional posture, leaning slightly forward toward camera showing engagement.

OUTFIT:
Ultra-professional high-end business attire. Tailored charcoal gray blazer with subtle sheen, perfect fit, structured shoulders. Crisp white silk blouse underneath, elegant neckline. Statement necklace (gold or pearl, sophisticated). Overall look: Executive, fashionable, expensive, commanding presence.

POSE & GESTURE:
Leaning forward toward camera (engaged, present). RIGHT HAND: Raised and pointing upward (index finger extended, directing attention to windows above), confident gesture. LEFT HAND: Holding elegant tablet or clipboard (modern but professional, visible in lower frame). Body language: Commanding, teaching, directing. Expression: Speaking, explaining, orchestrating - mouth slightly open mid-sentence.

SETTING - PROFESSIONAL HOME OFFICE:
Background: Soft bokeh blur (shallow depth of field, f/2.8). Hints of luxurious home office: elegant bookshelves with leather-bound books (out of focus), warm wood tones, sophisticated decor, perhaps plant or artwork. Warm ambient lighting creating professional atmosphere. No clutter, refined aesthetic.

LIGHTING:
Professional three-point lighting setup. KEY LIGHT: Soft ring light from front creating even, flattering illumination on face, catchlights in eyes. FILL LIGHT: Subtle from side reducing shadows. BACK LIGHT: Rim light separating her from background, creating depth. Color temperature: Warm (3200K) creating welcoming, professional atmosphere. Skin tones: Natural, healthy, glowing. No harsh shadows, perfect webcam lighting.

CAMERA:
Webcam perspective: Slightly elevated angle (10 degrees above eye level, flattering). Framing: Medium close-up showing head, shoulders, and upper torso. Composition: Centered but dynamic due to gesture. Shallow depth of field (f/2.8) with sharp focus on face and fox ears, background softly blurred. Lens: 50mm equivalent, natural perspective, no distortion.

EXPRESSION & ENERGY:
Confident, intelligent, commanding yet approachable. Teaching/directing expression - explaining complex concepts. Eyes showing focus and mastery. Slight smile conveying competence and warmth. Overall presence: Master orchestrator, supreme executive, in complete control of vast operations, charismatic leader, professional yet personable.

FOX EARS DETAIL:
Elegant and sophisticated (not cartoonish). Blonde fur matching hair color. Alert position, slightly forward-facing showing active engagement and listening. Subtle integration with human features. Realistic fur texture. Proportionate size (not oversized). Adds magical element while maintaining professional appearance.

ATMOSPHERE:
Professional, polished, executive presence. High-end business aesthetic. Warm, welcoming yet commanding. Sense of intelligence, capability, and authority. Perfect for IDE interface webcam feed - someone you'd trust to orchestrate complex business operations.

TECHNICAL QUALITY:
Photorealistic Unreal Engine 5 RTX rendering. Physically-based skin rendering with subsurface scattering. Realistic hair with individual strand detail. Accurate fabric rendering (silk, wool textures). Professional color grading. Cinematic quality portrait photography aesthetic.

artifact.image_name: Foxy IDE Interface - Sistema Completo de Orquestación

- TOP RIGHT WINDOW: Title bar "Sales & Marketing Network" with the third image (interconnected factory clusters, isometric view)

Lines originate from Foxy's position and extend to center of each window. BRIGHTEST line connects to Sales & Marketing window (top right) as Foxy is pointing toward it. Notification badges (small red circles with numbers) on windows requiring attention.

INTERFACE DETAILS:

MID-GROUND LEFT - SYSTEM MAINTENANCE WORKSHOP:

STATION 2: Preventive maintenance being performed, engineer oiling gears, tightening bolts, replacing worn parts. Parts inventory with labeled bins and drawers. Maintenance schedule board showing equipment and service dates. Work orders on clipboards. Spare parts storage with organized shelving.

MID-GROUND RIGHT - SECURITY & COMPLIANCE TOWER:

LEVEL 2: Surveillance station with guards monitoring multiple viewing ports (mechanical periscopes) showing different facility areas, taking notes on observation logs. LEVEL 3: Access control center managing locks and keys, master key storage in secure cabinet, lock testing station. LEVEL 4: Compliance office with auditors reviewing policy documents, checking certifications, maintaining regulatory compliance files. Security patrol routes marked on wall map. Incident response protocols posted. Alarm system with bells and signal flags.

Massive secure vault with reinforced doors (circular bank vault style, heavy metal, combination locks). Inside vault: Organized archive of backup records, duplicate files stored in fireproof cabinets, organized by date and system. Recovery procedures manual on stand. Backup verification station where clerks periodically test backup integrity by retrieving and checking samples. Disaster recovery supplies (emergency equipment, restoration tools). Climate control system maintaining optimal storage conditions (temperature and humidity gauges visible). Backup schedule calendar showing daily, weekly, monthly backup rotations. Off-site storage preparation area with sealed containers ready for transport.

LEFT SIDE - NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE HUB:
Central connection point for facility communications. Pneumatic tube network hub with tubes branching in all directions (like telephone switchboard). Operators routing messages to correct destinations by directing tubes with valve controls. Speaking tube switchboard with operators connecting calls between departments. Physical network map on wall showing all connections between buildings and departments. Cable management system with organized routing (represented as rope and pulley systems). Signal amplification stations boosting long-distance communications. Network monitoring station checking connection quality, testing signal strength with meters.

RIGHT SIDE - SYSTEM UPGRADE & INNOVATION LAB:
Research area for improving existing systems. Engineers testing new mechanical components and processes. Prototype testing stations with new designs being evaluated. Compatibility testing ensuring new components work with existing systems. Performance benchmarking comparing old vs new methods with side-by-side demonstrations. Documentation station creating technical manuals for new systems. Training materials being developed for system changes. Upgrade schedule planning board showing rollout timeline. Pilot testing area with small-scale implementation before full deployment.

CENTER - IT COMMAND CENTER:
Elevated control room overseeing all technology operations. IT director (wise owl in vest) monitoring status boards showing system health. Status indicators for each major system (green flags=operational, yellow=warning, red=down). Incident response team ready to deploy for problems. Priority queue board showing current issues and assignments. Communication hub coordinating between all technology areas. Performance metrics displayed on mechanical counters (uptime percentage, response times, capacity utilization). Strategic planning area with roadmap for technology improvements.

artifact.image_name: Mundo Adicional - Operaciones de Logística y Cadena de Suministro
artifact.prompt_text:
Photorealistic UE5 RTX render of Supply Chain & Logistics operations visualized as massive mechanical distribution and inventory management system.

FOREGROUND - RECEIVING & INSPECTION DOCK:
Large loading dock with multiple bays. Wooden carts and wagons arriving with raw materials and supplies. DOCK 1: Workers unloading crates and barrels, using hand trucks and dollies. DOCK 2: Inspection team checking deliveries against packing lists, examining quality with magnifying glasses. DOCK 3: Weighing station with large balance scales verifying quantities. DOCK 4: Sorting area directing materials to appropriate storage locations using color-coded tags. Clipboard inspectors checking off items on receiving documents. Damaged goods area for items requiring attention. Quarantine zone for items pending approval. Stamp station marking approved items with "RECEIVED" stamp.

MID-GROUND - AUTOMATED WAREHOUSE SYSTEM:
Massive multi-level storage facility with mechanical retrieval system. Floor-to-ceiling shelving units (30ft tall) organized in grid pattern with labeled aisles (A1, A2, B1, B2, etc.). Mechanical lift system (like elaborate elevator) moving vertically to access different levels. Horizontal conveyor tracks at each level transporting items. Robotic picker arms (mechanical, steam-powered, brass construction) retrieving items from shelves based on pick lists. Inventory tracking system with clerks maintaining location records in large ledger books. Barcode equivalent system using colored tags and reference charts. Cycle counting teams periodically verifying inventory accuracy with clipboards. Replenishment system monitoring stock levels with visual indicators (red flag=low stock, green=adequate). FIFO/LIFO management with date stamps on items ensuring proper rotation.

MID-GROUND RIGHT - ORDER FULFILLMENT CENTER:
Active picking and packing operation. ZONE 1: Order pickers with carts collecting items from warehouse based on pick lists, checking item numbers against orders. ZONE 2: Packing stations with workers wrapping items in protective materials (paper, cloth), placing in boxes, adding packing materials. ZONE 3: Labeling station applying shipping labels with destination information, using stamps and handwriting. ZONE 4: Quality check station verifying order accuracy, comparing packed items to order forms. ZONE 5: Staging area organizing packed orders by destination and shipping method. Conveyor belt system moving packed orders to shipping dock. Rush order express lane for priority shipments.

BACKGROUND - SHIPPING & DISTRIBUTION NETWORK:
Shipping dock with multiple departure bays. Packed orders being loaded onto delivery wagons and carts organized by route. Route planning station with large map showing delivery territories, route optimization using colored pins and string showing paths. Dispatch coordinator assigning deliveries to drivers, providing route sheets and manifests. Tracking system with departure log recording when shipments leave facility. Delivery confirmation system with returned signed receipts being filed. Fleet management area maintaining delivery vehicles, scheduling maintenance. Driver check-in station where drivers report back and provide delivery updates.

LEFT SIDE - INVENTORY CONTROL TOWER:
Elevated control center overseeing entire warehouse operation. Inventory manager (owl with spectacles) monitoring stock levels using visual dashboard (large board with product categories and quantity indicators). Reorder point system with automatic flags triggering when items reach minimum levels. Purchase order generation station creating requisitions for suppliers. Demand forecasting area analyzing trends using historical data charts and graphs. ABC analysis board categorizing inventory by importance (A=critical, B=important, C=routine). Slow-moving inventory identification system flagging items for clearance. Inventory valuation calculations using abacus and ledgers.

RIGHT SIDE - RETURNS & REVERSE LOGISTICS:
Returns processing center handling incoming returns. STATION 1: Receiving returned items, checking condition, documenting reason for return. STATION 2: Inspection and testing determining if items can be restocked, need repair, or must be scrapped. STATION 3: Restocking area returning acceptable items to warehouse inventory. STATION 4: Repair workshop fixing damaged items for resale. STATION 5: Disposal area for unsalvageable items, recycling materials when possible. Return authorization system approving refunds or exchanges. Customer communication station notifying customers of return status.

CENTER - SUPPLY CHAIN COORDINATION HUB:
Central command coordinating entire supply chain. Large planning board showing supply chain flow from suppliers through warehouse to customers. Supplier relationship management area maintaining vendor files and performance records. Lead time tracking showing expected arrival dates for incoming shipments. Bottleneck identification system highlighting delays or issues with red flags. Contingency planning area developing backup plans for disruptions. Cross-docking operations for items bypassing storage (direct transfer from receiving to shipping). Just-in-time coordination minimizing inventory holding. Collaboration station communicating with suppliers and customers via pneumatic tubes and messenger services.

INTERCONNECTIONS:
Receiving dock feeding into warehouse storage. Warehouse feeding into fulfillment center. Fulfillment feeding into shipping dock. Returns flowing back through system. Inventory control monitoring and directing all flows. Supply chain hub coordinating timing and quantities. Visible material flow throughout facility via conveyors, carts, and pneumatic tubes. Information flow via documents, messengers, and speaking tubes.

ATMOSPHERE: Efficient, organized, constant motion, sense of flow and rhythm, logistics precision, industrial warehouse aesthetic, steampunk-Victorian supply chain, natural materials (wood, metal, rope), mechanical automation, cool industrial lighting, visible inventory and material handling, systematic processes, scale and complexity of distribution operations.

- Dynamic energy, movement, urgency
- Professional competence under pressure
- Strategic thinking visible in her expression

LIGHTING:
Dramatic lighting on Foxy (spotlight effect, she's the focal point). Each window casting different colored light toward center (red from manufacturing crisis, green from sales success, etc.). Foxy illuminated by multiple colored lights showing she's receiving information from all sources. Rim lighting separating her from background. Heroic, powerful lighting emphasizing her command presence.

VISUAL COMPOSITION:
Foxy at center as anchor point. Eight windows arranged in dynamic circle around her at varying distances creating depth. Lines of communication (tubes, light beams, messenger paths) converging on her position. Radial composition emphasizing her as central hub. Sense of her as nexus of entire business empire. Clear visibility of all eight districts and their connections. Sense of vast, complex, but organized system. Living, breathing business ecosystem. Everything interconnected and flowing.

Magnificent business empire, comprehensive operations, systematic organization, visible integration, sense of scale and complexity, steampunk-Victorian aesthetic throughout, natural materials (brass, wood, stone, glass), mechanical precision, magical elements (Foxy's energy, messenger fairies), professional excellence, thriving enterprise, masterful orchestration, complete business ecosystem under single command.

text: ¡Increíble! Ahora voy a crear una última imagen mostrando una vista "behind the scenes" de cómo Foxy entrena a su equipo de gestoras para que puedan orquestar sus propios sub-mundos:

artifact.prompt_text:
Photorealistic UE5 RTX render of FOXY training her executive management team - diverse women with fox ears learning to orchestrate their own operational worlds.

CENTER FOREGROUND - FOXY AS MASTER INSTRUCTOR:
Stunning blonde FOXY (original, primary) standing at elegant podium/teaching station (brass and wood, Victorian design). Professional high-end business attire: charcoal blazer, white blouse, statement jewelry. TEACHING POSE: One hand gesturing toward large demonstration board behind her, other hand holding elegant pointer stick. Expression: Confident, knowledgeable, patient teacher. Fox ears alert and engaged. Speaking, mouth slightly open mid-explanation. Warm, encouraging smile. Body language: Open, welcoming, authoritative but approachable.

BEHIND FOXY - LARGE DEMONSTRATION BOARD:
Massive wooden board (12ft wide, 8ft tall) showing "The Art of Orchestration - Master Class". Detailed mechanical diagram showing how to manage multiple operational worlds simultaneously. Sections labeled:
- "Priority Assessment" (with decision tree flowchart)
- "Resource Allocation" (with hydraulic system diagram)
- "Crisis Management" (with emergency response protocols)
- "Interconnection Optimization" (with network flow charts)
- "Team Empowerment" (with delegation strategies)
Hand-drawn illustrations, annotations, color-coded systems. Professional teaching materials.

FOREGROUND - EIGHT STUDENT GESTORAS (diverse executive women):
Seated at elegant desks arranged in semi-circle facing Foxy. Each woman has elegant fox ears (different colors matching their ethnicity/style), professional high-end business attire, taking notes attentively.

STUDENT 1 (Front Left) - ASIAN GESTORA:
Beautiful Asian woman, elegant black fox ears, navy business suit, writing notes in leather journal with fountain pen, focused expression, glasses, hair in professional bun.

STUDENT 2 (Front Center-Left) - AFRICAN GESTORA:
Stunning Black woman, rich brown fox ears, burgundy blazer, gold jewelry, using brass mechanical calculator while taking notes, confident posture.

STUDENT 3 (Front Center) - LATINA GESTORA:
Gorgeous Latina woman, auburn fox ears, emerald green suit, reviewing miniature mechanical model on her desk (practice orchestration system), analytical expression.

STUDENT 4 (Front Center-Right) - MIDDLE EASTERN GESTORA:
Beautiful Middle Eastern woman, golden-brown fox ears, royal blue attire with elegant patterns, sketching orchestration diagrams, artistic approach to learning.

STUDENT 5 (Front Right) - SOUTH ASIAN GESTORA:
Striking Indian woman, sleek black fox ears, sophisticated purple suit, operating small practice control panel on desk, hands-on learner.

STUDENT 6 (Back Left) - EUROPEAN GESTORA:
Elegant European woman, silver-blonde fox ears, classic gray suit, comparing notes with neighbor, collaborative learner.

STUDENT 7 (Back Center) - EAST ASIAN GESTORA:
Beautiful Korean woman, white-tipped fox ears, modern tailored suit, using abacus for calculations, traditional meets modern approach.

STUDENT 8 (Back Right) - INDIGENOUS GESTORA:
Stunning Native American woman, russet fox ears with natural patterns, earth-toned professional attire with cultural elements, taking holistic notes connecting all concepts.

EACH DESK HAS:

- Note-taking materials (journals, pens, ink)
- Practice problems and case studies
- Personal communication devices (speaking tubes, message capsules)

MID-GROUND - PRACTICE STATIONS:
Behind the seated students: Eight individual practice booths where students can apply lessons. Each booth has:
- Small-scale operational world to manage (miniature factory, office, or system)
- Control panel with levers, switches, gauges
- Performance metrics showing how well they're managing
- Foxy's assistant instructors (experienced managers) providing guidance
- Real-time feedback systems showing results of their decisions

BACKGROUND - TRAINING ACADEMY ENVIRONMENT:
Elegant classroom in Foxy's executive academy. Tall windows with natural light. Bookshelves filled with business management texts and orchestration manuals. Wall displays showing:
- "Hall of Master Orchestrators" (portraits of successful graduates)
- "Current Empire Status" (live feed from actual operations)
- "Best Practices" (documented successful strategies)
- "Lessons Learned" (case studies of challenges overcome)
Sophisticated, professional learning environment. Warm wood tones, brass accents, comfortable but focused atmosphere.

LEFT SIDE - LIVE DEMONSTRATION AREA:
Transparent glass enclosure showing actual small-scale operational world in action. Students can observe real mechanical processes while learning theory. Currently showing: Miniature manufacturing line with tiny workers, allowing students to see cause-and-effect in real-time. Foxy occasionally pointing to this demonstration while explaining concepts.

RIGHT SIDE - COLLABORATION ZONE:
Area where students work together on group exercises. Two students currently collaborating on joint orchestration challenge - managing interconnected worlds together. Practicing coordination and communication. Building teamwork skills.

TEACHING MOMENT CAPTURED:
Foxy explaining: "When Manufacturing has a crisis (pointing to red alert on demonstration board), you must simultaneously assess impact on Supply Chain, communicate with Finance for emergency resources, and notify Customer Service of potential delays. Watch..." She activates demonstration showing cascade of decisions and their effects. Students leaning forward, captivated, some nodding in understanding, others taking rapid notes.

MAGICAL TEACHING ELEMENTS:
- Foxy's pointer creating light trails showing connections between concepts
- Small fairy assistants distributing practice materials to students
- Holographic (magical) projections showing 3D visualizations of complex systems
- Gentle golden glow around Foxy showing her mastery and teaching energy
- Students' fox ears perking up when they have "aha moments" (understanding)

ATMOSPHERE OF MENTORSHIP:
- Foxy as generous teacher sharing her expertise
- Students as eager, capable learners ready to lead
- Sense of knowledge transfer and empowerment
- Professional development and growth
- Building next generation of master orchestrators
- Diversity of approaches and perspectives enriching learning
- Supportive, collaborative learning environment
- High standards but encouraging atmosphere
- Practical, hands-on training combined with theory
- Real-world application focus

DETAILS SHOWING PROGRESSION:

- Others with "Junior Manager" designations
- Progress charts showing each student's development
- Graduation requirements posted: "Master 5 operational worlds simultaneously"
- Certification process outlined
- Career pathways displayed

EMOTIONAL TONE:
Inspiring, empowering, professional excellence, knowledge sharing, mentorship, growth, capability building, confidence development, collaborative learning, high standards, supportive environment, next generation leadership, diversity of talent, unified by common goal of mastery.

LIGHTING: Warm, welcoming classroom lighting (3500K). Natural light from windows. Focused task lighting on desks. Dramatic lighting on Foxy emphasizing her as master teacher. Each student well-lit showing their engagement and focus.

CAMERA: Wide shot showing entire classroom, slight elevation to see all students and Foxy, composition emphasizing Foxy as central teacher with students arranged around her, clear visibility of teaching materials and practice stations, sense of comprehensive training program.
